titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Pearl London Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday February 1, 2020 at 04:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection documents the life and career of American poet and teacher of poetry, Pearl London (1916-2003) and includes audiocassette recordings of conversations with poets; London's annotated books and related teaching materials; correspondence with poets; and some original manuscripts.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection includes audiocassette recordings of conversations with poets; London's annotated books and related teaching materials; correspondence with poets; and some original manuscripts.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, identified by a unique call number and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Pearl London (1916-2003) was an American poet and teacher of poetry.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 6-10 (Audiocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Pearl London Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, 2017.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Pearl London papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, Newspaper Clippings, Interview Transcripts, and other Teaching Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and works by James Merrill, Muriel Rukeyser, Derek Walcott, Stanley Plumly, Marine Kumine, Philip Levine, Marilyn Hacker, Amy Clampitt and Robert Haas.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, Newspaper Clippings, Interview Transcripts, and other Teaching Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and writings by Molly Peacock, Eamon Grennan, Louise Gluck, Charles Simic, June Jordan, William Matthews, C.K. Williams, Galway Kinnell, Edward Hirsch, Robert Pinsky, and Paul Muldoon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by David Kalstone, Hugh Seidman, Gregory Orr, Jane Cooper, James Merrill, June Jordan, William Heyen, Celia Gilbert, Louise Gluck, Linda Pastan, Stanley Plumly, Jean Valentine, Elizabeth Hardwick, Carol Muske, Marilyn Heller, Heather McHugh, Tess Gallagher, Robert Haas, and John Ashbery.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, Newspaper Clippings, Interview Transcripts, and other Teaching Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and writings by Ai, Jorie Graham, Paul Muldoon, Frank Bidart, and Jason Schinder.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, Newspaper Clippings, Interview Transcripts, and other Teaching Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and writings by Edward Hirsch W.S. Merwin, Eamon Grennan, Frak Bidart, Derek walcott, June Jordan, Stanley Plumly, Louis Simpson, Marge Piercy, Ruth Whitman, Paul Levin, Charles Simic, and Tess Gallagher.
